title: Occupational exposure to organic solvents during pregnancy and childhood behavior: findings from the PELAGIE birth cohort (France, 2002-2013).
ehr_evidence: The study uses a birth cohort (PELAGIE) with prospectively self-reported exposure data, not EHR.
summary: A longitudinal study of the PELAGIE birth cohort in France investigating the impact of prenatal occupational exposure to organic solvents on children's internalizing and externalizing behaviors at ages 2 and 6.
key_findings: Increased externalizing behavior at age 2 associated with prenatal solvent exposure.
The association was attenuated by age 6.
No association was observed for internalizing behavior.
population: Pregnant women and children in France
